name: Greenland
avoid: Inaccessible in harsh winter without expedition-level prep

cities.name: Nuuk
cities.notes: Colorful colonial harbour, Greenland National Museum and Inuit culture

cities.name: Ilulissat
cities.notes: UNESCO Icefjord with massive calving glacier and midnight sun hikes

cities.name: Sisimiut
cities.notes: Gateway to the Arctic Circle Trail 160km multi-day wilderness trek

cities.name: Kangerlussuaq
cities.notes: International gateway with musk ox country and ice sheet access

itinerary.sim: Tusass prepaid SIM — buy in Nuuk or Kangerlussuaq; Greenland is outside EU roaming and coverage is strongest in major towns.

Nuuk.name: Nuuk
Nuuk.note: Use Nuuk for flight recovery, museums, and one fjord day before heading deeper into Greenland; weather delays are common, so this is the best city for your first two nights.

activities.name: Colonial Harbour walk covering Hans Egede's House, the Mother of the Sea statue, Nuuk Cathedral, and the old waterfront boardwalk
Greenland National Museum for the Qilakitsoq mummies and Inuit cultural collections
Katuaq Cultural Centre architecture stop and exhibition browse
Self-guided Nuuk Art Walk through the compact city centre
Evening stroll by Nuuk Fjord and Circus Lake for Sermitsiaq views

days.theme: Nuuk Fjord Adventure & Seasonal Aurora Window

activities.name: Nuuk Fjord boat tour past iceberg passages and abandoned settlements
activities.tip: This is Nuuk's signature excursion; keep it on the clearest weather window.

activities.name: Quassussuaq (Lille Malene) hike for panoramic city and fjord views
Kittat Economusée to watch Greenlandic national costume craftsmanship
Guided paddleboarding among icebergs in sheltered harbour waters
Northern Lights drive or boat outing in winter months
activities.tip: Best from February to March when skies are dark but flights are still operating reliably.

Kangerlussuaq.name: Kangerlussuaq
Kangerlussuaq.note: Greenland's classic inland air hub is the easiest place to touch the ice sheet and spot musk oxen; one well-planned night is enough for most first-time trips.

days.theme: Russell Glacier, Ice Sheet Access & Tundra Wildlife

activities.name: Russell Glacier outing for a close look at the Greenland Ice Sheet edge
activities.tip: A guided transfer is easiest unless you are prepared for the long gravel-road approach.

activities.name: Musk ox and caribou tundra safari around Lake Ferguson and Black Ridge
Kangerlussuaq Museum for aviation, WWII, and settlement history
Northern Lights watch in winter or dog-sled / snowmobile add-on when snow conditions allow

Sisimiut.name: Sisimiut
Sisimiut.note: Sisimiut adds Greenlandic small-town life, Arctic Circle Trail scenery, and a strong culture stop without needing a long detour; it works best as a one-night pause between inland and Disko Bay sectors.

days.theme: Arctic Circle Trail Gateway & Inuit Heritage

activities.name: Sisimiut Museum for Inuit history, colonial buildings, and traditional kayak displays
Tele Island (Sallinnguit) walk for archaeology, harbour views, and an easy taste of the Arctic Circle Trail landscape
Taseralik Cultural Centre exhibitions and lakefront coffee stop
Hotel Sisimiut Arctic Spa & sauna or a short summer boat safari from the harbour
activities.tip: Pick the spa in shoulder seasons and the boat safari in July–August for the best value.

Ilulissat.name: Ilulissat
Ilulissat.note: Ilulissat is the star stop of a Greenland first trip; give it three nights so you can keep one full day flexible for weather-sensitive whale, boat, or helicopter excursions.

activities.name: Ilulissat Icefjord Centre for climate and glacier exhibits in the landmark waterfront building
Sermermiut boardwalk and viewpoint walk through the ancient settlement valley
Zion's Church waterfront stop and harbour photography session
Knud Rasmussen Museum for polar exploration history
Short guided city walk to learn sled-dog culture and local history

days.theme: Disko Bay Boat Day, Whales & Icebergs

activities.name: Whale-watching cruise in Disko Bay for humpback, fin, and minke sightings
activities.tip: July and August are the best months for humpback activity.

activities.name: Iceberg safari by boat among giant calved blocks from Sermeq Kujalleq
Kaffemik cultural visit with coffee, cake, and Greenlandic hospitality
Guided sea kayaking in the icefjord with dry suit and safety gear
Ilulissat Art Museum quick stop if weather closes in

days.theme: Midnight Sun / Aurora Flex Day & Scenic Flight Options

activities.name: Northern Lights outing in winter or a midnight-sun hike along the icefjord rim in summer
Traditional dog-sled tour on frozen terrain in winter months
Helicopter scenic flight over the glacier front and calving ice
Dog-feeding hour for an easy culture stop before departure day

connections.method: Air Greenland Dash-8 domestic flight (about 45–60 min)
Air Greenland domestic flight, usually about 45 min
Air Greenland coastal flight, often nonstop or with a short operational stop

extras: Book Air Greenland sectors 4–6 months ahead; summer seats and the best through-fares sell out early.
Keep at least one buffer half-day because fog and wind regularly delay domestic flights across Greenland.
Vegetarian food is limited outside Nuuk and Ilulissat, so request meals with hotels in advance and carry trail snacks.
Cards work in most towns, but carry some Danish kroner for kiosks, small cafés, and weather-disruption days.
June to August is best for boats, hiking, and whales; February to March is best for dog sledding and Northern Lights.
